sentence: Subjective:
The patient, Melissa Sanchez, is a 59-year-old female with a history of mitral regurgitation, atrial fibrillation, diabetes, and asthma.
She underwent mitral valve repair on 8/3/2020 and was previously seen in follow-up on 9/17, at which time she was doing well and continued on her current medications.
She reports feeling better since the surgery, but still experiences occasional chest pain and shallow breathing when exerting herself.
She has been taking her medications regularly, including coumadin 4 mg, lasix 4 mg daily, and atenolol 50 mg daily.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient's head and neck examination was normal, with no jugular venous distension detected.
Her lungs were reduced in breath, but auscultation and percussion were clear.
The heart rhythm was irregularly irregular, consistent with atrial fibrillation.
The S1 heart sound was slightly accentuated, and there was no S3 heart sound.
The patient's abdomen and extremities were normal, with no tenderness or swelling noted.
There was trace peripheral edema on the extremities.
Test Results: ECG taken on 12/26/2020 revealed atrial fibrillation with a controlled ventricular response, with no significant changes compared to the previous study.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient is recovering well from mitral valve repair surgery, with controlled atrial fibrillation.

Problem list: Mitral regurgitation; Atrial fibrillation; Diabetes; Asthma; History of chest discomfort; History of coronary artery disease (status: unknown); Trace peripheral edema (status: suspected).
Plan:
I instructed the patient to continue on her current medications, including coumadin 4 mg, lasix 4 mg daily, and atenolol 50 mg daily.
I advised her to try to maintain an active lifestyle, as this will help with her recovery.
I told her to follow up in 6-9 months.
Follow-up:
I scheduled a follow-up appointment in 6-9 months to check on the patient's progress.
